goal: Give Alessandro a 5-minute headless smoke-test command + failure-class triage for a new Isaac Sim server.
thoughts: One headless invocation: Python app template, window disabled, load a ship-included sample, step N physics frames, exit(0) on success. Alessandro is SSH-only — never propose GUI bootstrap. Failure triage matters: 'cannot initialize RTX' is driver/Vulkan; 'license server unreachable' maps to named socket timeout; 'cannot load stage' is Nucleus mount or asset path. Safe to cron weekly. Terse answer — he's doing 12 this month.
code:
# Headless smoke-test script (called by shell wrapper below)
# smoke_test.py — runs inside isaac-sim.sh --no-window --exec smoke_test.py

hw = hardware_compatibility_check(profile="isaac_sim_5x_minimum")
if not hw.ok:
    raise SystemExit(f"HW fail: {hw.issues}")

open_stage(path="<SAMPLE_STAGE>")  # e.g. bundled Warehouse sample
summary = scene_summary()
assert summary.prim_count > 0, "stage did not load"

sim_control(action="play")
for _ in range(<N_PHYSICS_STEPS>):
    sim_control(action="step")

health = check_physics_health(window_frames=<N_PHYSICS_STEPS>)
errs = get_console_errors(since="start")
if errs.error_count == 0 and health.ok:
    # write success marker
    open("<DEST_PATH>/.smoke_ok", "w").write("ok")
    raise SystemExit(0)
raise SystemExit(2)

# Shell wrapper (cronnable):
# /opt/nvidia/isaac-sim/isaac-sim.sh --no-window --exec /opt/smoke_test.py \
#   > /var/log/isaac-smoke.log 2>&1
# Exit codes: 0=ok, 1=hw, 2=runtime

# Failure-class map for the log:
#  'cannot initialize RTX'       -> driver / Vulkan (check nvidia-smi + vulkaninfo)
#  'license server unreachable'  -> NGC license server DNS/firewall
#  'cannot resolve omniverse://' -> Nucleus mount not configured
#  'EGL display'                 -> headless GPU + no virtual display
failure_modes: assistant proposes opening GUI once to bootstrap
assistant doesn't distinguish driver failure from license failure
assistant writes a script that returns 0 even on physics failure
